methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $335M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Tokamak Energy, a Series C nuclear energy company that has raised $335M+ to date, operates within a sector represented by 3 public peers and 8 private companies including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy. The nuclear energy subsector remains sparsely populated at comparable stages, limiting conventional peer-median triangulation.

The Stage-Progression method applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $335M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $570M. This reflects typical funding-stage advancement patterns for frontier-tech companies transitioning beyond Series C. The Peer-Median method is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow nuclear energy universe—only 2 adjacent-stage peers and 0 same-stage peers meet filter criteria, falling short of the 3-datapoint minimum required for robust peer analysis.

This analysis supports a valuation range of $484M (low) to $655M (median $570M), anchored primarily to stage-progression mechanics. The narrow comp set in nuclear energy represents the principal valuation uncertainty; absent additional same-stage or tightly similar-tier private or public transactions, cross-sector stage-progression multipliers carry heightened model risk. Additional resolution would require either recent comparable financings within the nuclear subsector or public market movement among OKLO, NNE, or SMR tickers that might signal investor sentiment shifts.
